text: Would you please hold each other's hands, signifying to all that now you are in each other's safekeeping.
Marriage is a beautiful blending of two hearts, two souls.
This joining together is the ultimate intimacy that two human beings can share.
It is an estate not to be entered into lightly, but rather to be approached reverently, sincerely, and with the deepest humility.
Love is friendship that has caught fire, sustained by giving yourselves to this sacred union.
And as you grow together, remember to sow and reap love with gratitude.
For each act of kindness, each tender smile, is a seed that will blossom forth to fill all of your tomorrows with peace, love, and harmony.
A man who truly understood love once said,
Love is patient, love is kind, and envies no one.
Love is never boastful, nor conceited, nor rude, never selfish, nor quick to take offense.
Love keeps no score of wrongs, does not gloat over others' errors, but delights in the truth.
There is nothing that love cannot face.
There is no limit to its faith, its hope, and its endurance.
Through these qualities of love, your knowledge and understanding of each other will deepen the bond between you.
Deep knowledge of one another is not something that can be achieved in a short time,
and real understanding of the other's feelings can develop fully only with years of intimacy.
It grows out of a deep caring to share your innermost selves,
to be open and vulnerable and supportive of each other,
not only in your joys and sorrows, but also in your successes.
Recognize that the events that fill your days,
though they may appear to be struggles and infirmities,
are but guideposts assisting each of you into love's deepest ways.
To know each other in this way is a priceless gift.
Give yourselves in love, but do not give yourselves away.
Khalil Gibran echoes these sentiments in *The Prophet* when he said,
Love one another, but make not a bond of love.
Let it rather be a moving sea between the shores of your souls.
Fill each other's cup, but drink not from the same cup.
Give one another of your bread, but eat not from the same loaf.
Sing and dance together, yet let each of you be alone,
though they quiver with the same music.
Thus it is out of the resonance between individuality and union
that love, whose incredible strength is born and reborn through all the years of your lives.
It is going to be a wonderful life for both of you standing side by side.
The future for you both is now ahead.
The past is gone, and today is the beginning of your tomorrow.
So your faith in each other must be flawless,
and your love for each other well with understanding and compassion.
So let us pray.
O loving presence of God,
we have come together this day to present these thy children
that they may be joined in the covenant of holy matrimony.
God, if they are protected by your love,
they shall be sustained.
Enlarge their hearts and open their eyes
so that they may approach their lives with tenderness and with wisdom.
Enrich them with thy grace
that they may be strong enough to overcome the trials of life together.
And so it is.
Amen.
And on this basis, Jim,
do you take Linda,
whose hand you hold,
to be your wife,
your companion,
and the partner of your joys and sorrows,
and shall it be your chief desire
to make her heart glad from this day forward?
And do you pledge yourself before God and these witnesses
to love, honor, cherish, and protect her
in sickness and in health,
in prosperity and in adversity,
and choosing her above all others,
keep only unto her so long as you both shall live.
And do you, Linda,
take Jim,
whose hand you hold,
to be your husband,
your companion,
the partner of your joys and sorrows,
and shall it be your chief desire
to make his heart glad from this day forward?
And do you pledge yourself before God and these witnesses
to love, honor, cherish, and protect him
in sickness and in health,
in prosperity and in adversity,
and choosing him above all others,
keep only unto him so long as you both shall live.
And of course,
the ring has been a symbol of marriage
down throughout the ages,
uniting man and woman
into the holy bonds of matrimony.
The ring is a visible symbol
of an invisible promise
both of you are making
from your hearts this day.
May I have the rings, please?
Mother, Father God, Lord Jesus Christ, we ask your blessing upon these rings
that they may always remind Linda and Jim of the promise they are making this day.
May these rings say to all that your commitment, Jim, and your commitment, Linda, is deep and everlasting.
And as you place these rings upon each other's fingers,
may this commitment grow and mature and deepen the wellspring of love flourishing in your hearts this day.
Thus you each are to wear your ring as the enclosing bond of reverence and trust.
Thus you both are to fulfill the perfect circle of love. And so Jim and Linda, having exchanged the vows of matrimony,
and having symbolized the same with the giving and receiving of rings, you stand before me
in ordaining the ministry of the Church of Truth, and recognizing the authority of the
laws of the State of California, I now pronounce you husband and wife.
As earthly marriage is symbolic of the mystical union between Christ and his Church, so now
we symbolize the union formed today by Jim and Linda by the lighting of a wedding candle.
The two lighted candles represent the lives each of you have lived as independent persons
up to this time. To mark the creation of your new union, would the two of you now take
the lighted candles and together light the center candle, and replace the two candles still
lit at the side to signify the maintaining of your individuality within this union.
The Lord bless you and keep you.
The Lord make his face to shine upon you and be gracious unto you.
The Lord lift up his countenance upon you and give you peace.
I bless this marriage in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit.
And so it is. In the name of Jesus Christ, amen.
You may now kiss your bride.
All right, you two.
Ladies and gentlemen, it is my pleasure and privilege
to present to you Mr. and Mrs. James Callahan.
Thank you.
